ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for roti prata with egg, onion and cheese filling. Mapped to the nearest parent class 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_03315763) as an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for stuffed roti prata. Code A00LC (Flat bread) used as the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for roti prata with egg, onion and cheese filling as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of egg, onion and cheese prata is approximately 120 g (one piece), providing around 298 kcal, 13.4 g protein, 17.2 g fat, and 22.4 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Prata (Egg, Onion, Cheese) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Micronutrients (calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) were present in the input record attributed to a delivery platform source; these have been retained at face value but flagged at lower confidence (0.70) and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimates for plausibility. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on plain roti / flatbread (wheat flour base, GI ~62)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for egg-onion-cheese prata in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 62 is estimated from the closest analogous food: plain roti / wheat-flour flatbread (GI ~62, medium category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The egg, onion and cheese filling does not substantially alter the GI of the wheat-flour base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 22.44) / 100 ≈ 13.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 450 mg per 100 g with minimal potassium and fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: At 450 mg sodium per 100 g, a single 120 g prata piece delivers approximately 540 mg sodium — over one-third of the 1500 mg daily target for hypertensive individuals — and should be consumed sparingly, with curry dipping sauce avoided or minimised to prevent further sodium loading.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour base with medium GI (~62) and low dietary fibre (1.03 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined flour base produces a medium glycaemic response (GI ~62); individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to one piece, pair with a high-fibre vegetable side, and avoid sugary dipping sauces to moderate postprandial blood glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.0 g per 100 g, trans fat 0.17 g per 100 g, and high sodium 450 mg per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat from cheese and ghee (4.0 g/100 g), trace trans fat (0.17 g/100 g), and high sodium (450 mg/100 g) elevates cardiovascular risk with regular consumption; individuals with CVD should limit intake to occasional servings and request preparation with minimal ghee and reduced-fat cheese.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prata with Egg, Onion and Cheese (also known as Roti Prata Telur Bawang Keju) is a popular Singaporean Indian flatbread made from a laminated wheat-flour dough that is stretched, folded with ghee, and pan-fried on a flat griddle until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft and layered inside. The filling consists of beaten egg, diced onion, and melted cheese — typically cheddar or processed cheese slices — which are enclosed within the folded dough before frying. The result is a savoury, slightly crispy flatbread with a rich, eggy, mildly cheesy interior and a sweet-savoury note from the cooked onion. It is a staple breakfast and supper dish at Indian Muslim hawker stalls and mamak restaurants across Singapore and Malaysia, commonly served with fish or mutton curry, dhal, or sambal for dipping. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 248 kcal, 11.2 g protein, 14.3 g fat (4.0 g saturated), 18.7 g carbohydrate, 1.03 g dietary fibre, and 450 mg sodium. The cheese and egg contribute calcium (83 mg/100 g), iron (0.83 mg/100 g), cholesterol (67 mg/100 g), and a modest amount of vitamin C (1.7 mg/100 g) from the onion. The dish is halal and vegetarian but contains gluten, egg, and dairy allergens. Clinically, the high sodium content (450 mg/100 g) is a concern for hypertensive individuals, the medium-GI refined flour base warrants portion control for those with type 2 diabetes, and the saturated fat and sodium profile requires moderation for cardiovascular disease management.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore at confidence 0.95 — an exact match for this dish. Micronutrients (calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) retained from input at 0.70 confidence and cross-checked for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimates. GI estimated from the closest analogous approved-source food (wheat-flour flatbread, GI ~62, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021).
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific egg-onion-cheese prata preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. Calcium (83.33 mg/100g), iron (0.83 mg/100g), cholesterol (66.67 mg/100g), saturated fat (4.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.17 g/100g), and vitamin C (1.67 mg/100g) were present in the input record attributed to a delivery platform source (grabfood, confidence 0.70); these values are plausible when cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimates for egg-stuffed flatbread and have been retained at 0.70 confidence. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on plain wheat-flour flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for egg-onion-cheese prata exists in the approved GI database. Portion label '1 Dosa' in the input record appears to be a data-entry error (prata is not a dosa); serving size of 120 g (one piece) used instead, consistent with typical hawker prata portion weights. All core macros (protein_g 11.16, fat_g 14.3, carbohydrate_g 18.7) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
